name: BMW M3 (E46)
reliability: 7/10 - the S54 is one of the all-time-great naturally aspirated inline-sixes and the chassis is fundamentally sound, but two known failures define ownership: rod bearings and rear subframe cracking. Address those and an E46 M3 is dependable; ignore them and it bites.
cost_to_own: Insurance: High (appreciating modern classic) · Maintenance ~$1,500/yr - budget for a preventive rod-bearing job and a subframe inspection on top of routine service.
popular_mods: Headers + tune - the classic NA wake-up for the S54
Preventive rod-bearing replacement (do it before chasing power)
Coilovers, sway bars, and a big brake kit
Supercharger kit for a big jump in power
Subframe reinforcement plates at the chassis mounts
oil_type: 10W-60 full synthetic (BMW M-spec; the S54 requires it)

generations.E30 (1986-1991): The original M3. 2.3L S14 four-cylinder homologation special, built for touring-car racing.
generations.E36 (1992-1999): 3.0/3.2L inline-six. US cars got a detuned engine versus Europe; still a sweet-handling coupe.
generations.E46 (2000-2006): This car. 3.2L S54 inline-six, 333 hp, 8,000 rpm redline - a high point of the breed. 6-speed manual or the clunky SMG. Watch rod bearings and rear subframe cracking.
generations.E90/E92/E93 (2008-2013): 4.0L S65 V8, 414 hp, 8,400 rpm - the only V8 M3. Rod bearings and throttle actuators are the weak spots.
generations.F80 (2014-2018): 3.0L S55 twin-turbo inline-six. First turbo M3; infamous crank-hub slip.
generations.G80 (2021-present): 3.0L S58 twin-turbo inline-six, up to 503 hp, optional xDrive AWD.

common_issues: Rod bearing wear (S54 factory clearances are tight) - preventive replacement is the accepted fix
Rear subframe mounting points crack where they meet the chassis
VANOS wear - rattle, rough idle, lost low-end response
SMG hydraulic pump and actuator failures on SMG cars
Aging plastic cooling-system parts (expansion tank, water pump)
maintenance_tips: Budget for a preventive rod-bearing replacement and inspect bearings for wear
Inspect the rear subframe mounts for cracks; reinforce if needed
Rebuild or refresh VANOS if you hear the telltale rattle
Use the specified 10W-60 and don't stretch oil intervals
Replace cooling-system components proactively before they strand you
